action.message: When the same behavioral correction appears more than 3 times across sessions, stop recording it as a preference and instead escalate to mechanical enforcement (a hook, a pre-tool gate, or a hard block) — advisory repetition is the diagnostic signal that the fix is at the wrong layer.
Always treat prior-turn state (approvals, context, access patterns) as expired by default — re-derive from the canonical source each time rather than inheriting from memory or convention.
